text.title: API Documentation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every api-documentation artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Reference MUST contain all six sections: Overview, Authentication, Quick Start, Endpoints, Error Codes, Changelog. Missing any section is invalid.

rationale.source: Stripe / Twilio / GitHub docs conventions + Internal — abandonment audit 2024

rule.rationale: Each section answers a specific evaluator question; missing sections force the evaluator to leave the docs to find the answer.

rule.statement: Quick Start MUST include at least one runnable curl example that succeeds with a real (test) token. Pseudo-curl is forbidden.
rule.rationale: First copy-paste win predicts integration conversion; non-runnable examples are negative signal.

rule.statement: Error Codes section MUST list every documented status code with a stable type URI (RFC 7807) AND link to the Problem Details schema in the OpenAPI spec.
rule.rationale: Programmatic clients key off type URIs; without a stable table they hard-code on title strings that change.

rule.statement: Endpoint examples in docs MUST be generated from the OpenAPI examples block, not hand-maintained. Drift between docs and spec is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hand-maintained examples diverge within weeks; codegen keeps the docs as a view on the spec.

rule.statement: Changelog MUST have one entry per spec_version bump with breaking-change flag + migration link.
rule.rationale: Partners plan around deprecations; without a Changelog they discover deprecations in production.
